scriptId: cs_v3_ACCUMULATION_L40_Relationship with a boss or coworker
historyId: h_v3_ACCUMULATION_L40_Relationship with a boss or coworker

goal: Get direct and practical guidance on communicating with a coworker about a workplace issue.
Incorporate remote-working considerations into guidance for communicating about workplace issues.
Provide polite follow-up templates after meetings where a coworker's point wasn't acknowledged.
Provide proven approaches for delivering upward feedback that avoids insubordination.
Offer guidance on starting and maintaining a lightweight, selective running log.
Provide a practical log template and guidelines for storage, tone, and escalation readiness.
Develop guidance on ensuring attribution and recognition for ideas in meetings and appropriate escalation if attribution is overlooked.
Provide tailored guidance for hybrid/remote teams to maintain visibility and communication effectiveness.
constraints: Evaluator role controls reviews, assignments, and potential career outcomes; approach must be strategic, cautious, and documentation-oriented.
Follow formal channels for issues involving legal, safety, discrimination, or retaliation.
Hybrid work constraints: limited informal face-time; guidance should address compensating strategies.
decisions: Escalation path: consider skip-level conversation with manager, HR contact, or ombudsperson if issues persist or involve harassment, retaliation, or policy violation.
Default to private one-on-one meetings when addressing concerns with an evaluator.
When possible, schedule key visibility activities on in-person office days to counter reduced informal face-time.
open: Define criteria for when to involve HR versus informal resolution
Explain how remote working style factors into approach, channels, and timing when addressing workplace issues
Define criteria for starting and maintaining a running log (scope, level of detail, retention).
Establish secure storage and privacy guidelines for the log and how it can be used in escalation or reviews.
Clarify how logging interacts with escalation paths and HR involvement.
Define criteria for attribution and recognition concerns in meetings (when to surface, who to inform, and escalation triggers).
Define how timestamped notes (e.g., emails) carry weight as evidence for attribution, including how to attach, quote, and log them, and when to surface them in private 1:1s or escalation.
Define criteria for in-meeting corrections: when to correct omissions in a public forum versus private follow-ups, including documentation and record-keeping guidelines.
Define recommended structure for performance evidence: lead with measurable results, timestamped evidence, and a one-line template per example.
Decide on the number of relational examples to include in reviews or logs (recommended range: 1–3).
Define an approach for crafting one-line templates for each example (Date | Situation | Action | Impact | Evidence | Corroboration).
Define guidance on audience-tailored messaging: adjust emphasis on metrics versus relational influence depending on reviewer priorities.
Define best practices for maintaining visibility and rapport in hybrid teams with limited informal face-time (e.g., optimized in-person days, structured check-ins, and async updates).
Define mechanisms to measure and improve informal knowledge transfer and relationship-building in hybrid contexts.
